safe_checks: After a qualifying push_clk edge with write-side reset intent active, normal write-side operation is reset-blocked because push_rst and push_sender_in_reset form the write-side reset condition and reset overrides writes.
After a qualifying pop_clk edge with pop-side reset active, normal read-side dequeue behavior is reset-blocked because pop_rst overrides pop-side operation.

Because push_rst and pop_rst are synchronous and internally registered, strict reset-state checks must be made only after the one-cycle delayed reset effect in the corresponding clock domain, not immediately when reset is driven high.
push_receiver_in_reset must indicate write-side reset state when the write side is in reset.
A pop transaction is counted only on a pop_clk edge where pop_valid && pop_ready is high.
If pop_valid is high and pop_ready is low across consecutive pop_clk cycles, pop_data must remain unchanged and the held item must not be removed.
The sequence of completed pop handshakes must equal the sequence of accepted pushed data values in strict FIFO order.
No value may be popped more times than it was accepted, and accepted values may not disappear without a matching pop handshake.
push_full indicates no available space, so once the design is observably full the testbench may strictly require that additional pushes are not treated as newly accepted until space is freed.
pop_empty indicates no data available, so in stable empty states after reset/drain it is safe to require pop_empty=1.
A successful pop eventually corresponds to exactly one one-cycle push_credit pulse in the push_clk domain unless pulse emission is being stalled by push_credit_stall.
While push_credit_stall is high, absence of push_credit pulses is allowed, but the cumulative number of observed push_credit pulses must never exceed the number of completed pops.
When valid data is persistently offered to the pop side and not consumed, the same pop_data value must continue to be offered until the handshake completes.
unsafe_checks: Do not strictly require push_valid alone to imply write acceptance under all credit/full conditions.
Do not strictly require a fixed push_clk latency from pop handshake to push_credit pulse.
Do not strictly require delayed credits during push_credit_stall to replay after an exact number of cycles.
Do not strictly require exact values or exact cycle-by-cycle updates of push_slots, credit_count_push, credit_available_push, or pop_items.
Do not strictly require exact cut-through timing of pop_valid or pop_data across asynchronous push/pop clock interactions.
Do not strictly require pop_empty to be the exact logical inverse of pop_valid in all boundary and cut-through situations.
Do not strictly require exact full/empty/status behavior during simultaneous push/pop activity when synchronizer lag may exist.
Do not strictly require exact arithmetic semantics of credit_available_push when credit_withhold_push exceeds credit_count_push.
Do not strictly require push_receiver_in_reset to assert or deassert combinationally with reset intent before the qualifying push_clk edge.
Do not strictly require writes attempted while full to be ignored versus stalled versus otherwise handled, beyond checking that no new accepted data enters the FIFO ordering stream.
Do not strictly require same-cycle reset-output behavior when reset overlaps active transfers beyond the delayed synchronous-reset rule.
Do not strictly require exact remote-domain visibility timing for pop_empty, push_full, or any CDC-derived counter/status output.
minimal_safe_oracle: Reset layer: verify one-cycle-delayed synchronous reset behavior separately in push and pop domains, including that push_sender_in_reset participates in the write-side reset condition and that push_receiver_in_reset indicates reset state after the qualifying push_clk edge.
FIFO ordering layer: build a reference queue from accepted writes under clearly non-full conditions and require that each completed pop handshake returns the oldest queued byte in order.
Backpressure layer: once a value is visible with pop_valid=1, hold pop_ready=0 for multiple pop_clk cycles and strictly require pop_data stability and no dequeue until pop_ready returns high.

Empty/drain layer: after draining all previously accepted data and allowing state to settle, require stable empty indication through pop_empty=1 and no further completed pops without a new accepted push.

Credit safety layer: count completed pops and observed push_credit pulses, require each pulse to be exactly one push_clk cycle wide, require pulses never to outnumber pops, and allow arbitrary delay especially while push_credit_stall=1.

reset_tests.description: Push-domain synchronous reset timing. Start idle with push_valid=0, pop_ready=0, push_sender_in_reset=0. Drive push_rst=1 before a push_clk edge and verify that write-side reset effects are checked only after the next push_clk edge, not immediately. After that qualifying edge, require push_receiver_in_reset to indicate reset and treat pushes as blocked.

Write-side reset via push_sender_in_reset OR path. Keep push_rst=0, assert push_sender_in_reset=1 across a push_clk edge, and verify the same reset-blocked behavior and reset indication as for push_rst. This discriminates mutants that ignore push_sender_in_reset or fail to OR it with push_rst.

Pop-domain synchronous reset timing. After previously making data available, drive pop_rst=1 before a pop_clk edge and verify strict reset checks only after the next pop_clk edge. Do not require immediate clearing on assertion. After the qualifying edge, do not expect normal dequeue behavior until reset clears.

Reset priority over normal operation. Present push_valid=1 with nonzero push_data while asserting push_rst or push_sender_in_reset through the qualifying push_clk edge, and separately present pop_ready=1 while asserting pop_rst through the qualifying pop_clk edge. Strict expectation is reset blocking, not transfer completion.

Reset deassert sequencing. Deassert push_rst and push_sender_in_reset, then allow one additional push_clk edge before relying on normal push-side behavior. Likewise deassert pop_rst and allow one additional pop_clk edge before relying on normal pop-side behavior. This targets mutants that clear reset too early or too late.

normal_tests.description: Single push then single pop in quiescent conditions. Use credit configuration that should not artificially restrict capacity, keep push_credit_stall=0, push one byte while not full, then later assert pop_ready when data is available. Expect one completed pop with exactly the pushed byte.
Multi-item FIFO ordering with no overlap. Push four distinct bytes in order, then pop four times after they are all known accepted. Expect exact order preservation.

Backpressure hold behavior. Make one item available to the pop side, then keep pop_ready=0 across multiple pop_clk cycles while pop_valid remains asserted. Require pop_data to remain constant and the item to be returned exactly once when pop_ready is finally raised.

Drain to empty and verify no extra output item appears. Push two bytes, pop both, then continue toggling pop_clk with pop_ready=1 for several cycles. Expect exactly two completed pops and no third phantom dequeue.

Credit pulse basic legality. Perform two successful pops with push_credit_stall=0 and observe push_clk long enough afterward. Expect exactly two one-cycle push_credit pulses eventually, with no pulse count exceeding completed pop count at any earlier time.

corner_case_tests.description: Depth boundary at 17 entries. In a no-pop phase, attempt to push 17 distinct bytes under permissive credit configuration, then attempt an 18th push. Strictly check ordering of the first 17 bytes when later popped and require that the 18th attempted write does not appear as an extra accepted item before space is freed. This targets 16-versus-17 depth bugs and off-by-one full handling.

Boundary transition 0 to 1 to 0. Starting from a drained stable empty state, push one byte, hold it under backpressure for several pop_clk cycles, then consume it and return to empty. Safe strict checks are FIFO ordering, hold stability, and stable empty indication after drain; avoid same-cycle cut-through truth-table assumptions.

Overlapping push and pop activity on independent clocks. Interleave pushes and pops with relatively prime push_clk and pop_clk periods so operations overlap. Strict check is that completed pop order exactly matches accepted push order with no duplication or loss.

Credit stall gating. Complete several pops while push_credit_stall=1 and observe that push_credit pulses may be absent during stall. Then deassert stall and continue observing. Safe strict checks: no pulses during stall are acceptable, cumulative pulses must never exceed completed pops, and eventual replay timing must not be fixed by the checker.
Data pattern boundaries for storage and stale-output bugs. Use all-zeros, all-ones, alternating, and high-bit-only bytes in one run to catch bit-slice, stale-data, and wrong-source mutants.

sequence_tests.description: Full reset-to-run traversal in both domains. Sequence: idle, assert push_rst, verify delayed push reset state, deassert and recover; push data; assert pop_rst while data exists, verify delayed pop reset state, deassert and recover; then consume data. This exposes wrong reset edge, missing internal registration, and reset priority mistakes.

Fill, partial drain, refill, full drain. Push to near/full capacity, pop a subset, push additional bytes, then drain completely. The expected-state trace for the reference queue is explicit at each accepted push and pop. This is the primary sequence for depth off-by-one, pointer wrap, and ordering bugs.

Backpressure persistence sequence. Push three bytes, allow first byte to become visible, hold pop_ready=0 for several pop_clk cycles, then release for one pop, hold again on the second byte, then fully drain. The explicit expected trace is that each held front item remains unchanged until its handshake and that later items do not advance early.

Credit accounting sequence without fixed latency assumptions. Perform a known number of successful pops under no stall, then another group under stall, then release stall. Track only the relation between completed pops and observed one-cycle push_credit pulses. This catches credits-on-push, duplicate-credit, missing-stall-gating, and never-returned-credit mutants.

Asynchronous overlap stress sequence. Use mismatched clocks, alternate bursts of push_valid and pop_ready, and include occasional independent resets separated by recovery windows. Maintain an explicit transaction queue and verify only guaranteed invariants: ordering, no loss, no duplication, held data stability, and reset blocking after the qualifying edge.

random_tests.description: Constrained-random dual-clock test with random push_valid, pop_ready, push_data, push_credit_stall, occasional push_rst, occasional pop_rst, and occasional push_sender_in_reset. Use independent randomized push_clk/pop_clk phase relationships. Score only safe invariants: delayed synchronous reset handling, FIFO order of accepted transactions, no dequeue without pop_valid&&pop_ready, pop_data stability under backpressure, no duplication/loss, one-cycle push_credit pulse width, and cumulative push_credit pulses never exceeding completed pops. Bias generation toward boundary occupancy regions near empty and near 17 entries, long pop_ready=0 stalls, and repeated data values mixed with distinct bytes.

key_discriminators: push_sender_in_reset must participate in the write-side reset OR path; a mutant that keys only on push_rst will fail the dedicated reset-origin test.
Reset effects are delayed by one domain clock because resets are synchronous and internally registered; asynchronous-reset or zero-latency-reset mutants will fail.
The FIFO depth is 17, not 16; the fill-to-17 then attempt-18 scenario separates correct capacity handling from common off-by-one full bugs.
Completed pop order must exactly match accepted push order under independent clocks; pointer or CDC ordering bugs fail the overlap sequences.
When pop_valid=1 and pop_ready=0, pop_data must hold constant and the item must not be removed; mutants with stale-output advancement or premature dequeue fail.
push_credit is caused by successful pops, not by pushes; credits-on-write mutants fail the credit accounting sequence.
push_credit_stall must suppress or pause credit pulse emission; mutants that ignore stall or emit pulses continuously fail.
Each push_credit pulse must be one push_clk cycle wide; stuck-high or multi-cycle credit pulse mutants fail without assuming any fixed return latency.
After draining all accepted data, no extra pop may occur; duplication, phantom-data, and failed-empty-handling mutants fail.
Using diverse byte patterns such as 0x00, 0xFF, 0xAA, 0x55, 0x80, and 0x01 exposes wrong bit slicing, inversion, and stale-data selection bugs.

The plan deliberately avoids exact cycle checks for cut-through timing and CDC-derived counters, reducing false failures from ambiguous spec areas while still discriminating protocol-breaking mutants.
